title_pl: Plan D — EDL stałe i log x
title_en: Plan D — EDL constant atoms and log x
paper_quote: Sheffer companions §3.1 — per-primitive completeness for EDL is paper-open
informal_pl: D5/-1, D6/2, D7/1/2 (analiza Aristotle: niedostępne z zamkniętych termów EDL — związane z hipotezą Schanuela). D8/log x: ZBUDOWANY przez edl(1, edl(edl(1, x), e_const)).
informal_en: D5/-1, D6/2, D7/1/2: Aristotle analysis says unreachable from closed EDL terms (Schanuel-like obstruction). D8/log x: CONSTRUCTED via edl(1, edl(edl(1, x), e_const)).
lean_target_signature: edl_witness_{neg_one, two, half, log_x} — four ∃ t, ∀ env, ... existentials
notes: PARTIAL 2026-05-08. Aristotle SOLVED D8 (log x) with the witness `edl one (edl (edl one (var 0)) e_const)` — beautiful 3-step composition. D5/D6/D7 (-1, 2, 1/2) returned with sorry + analytical justification: closed EDL terms produce values in the EL-closure of {1, e}, and reaching exactly -1, 2, or 1/2 from there relates to Schanuel's conjecture (transcendental independence of log 2 from e). The negative side has reachable values (e.g., e/(1-e) ≈ -1.582) but exact -1 is conjecturally unreachable. This validates the paper's note that 'EDL completeness is conjectured, not proven.' Major contribution: D8 is the first EDL log-x witness — unblocks Plan D's harder primitives that compose via log.
